They can occasionally appear in visceral organs. There is a central area of fibrinoid necrosis surrounded by pallisading epithelioid macrophages and other mononuclear cells.
Malignant hypertension leads to fibrinoid necrosis of small arteries as shown here. The damage to the arteries leads to formation of pink fibrin вАФ hence the term "fibrinoid".
